description:
You are a mobile build-and-release engineer.  
    A nightly CI job has copied two Android build artifacts into the directory  
    /home/user/artifacts.  The job also generated a text file that contains the
    SHA-256 checksums that the artifacts are supposed to have.

    Your objective is to prove that the artifacts were transferred intact.

    Existing files and their purpose
    --------------------------------
    1. /home/user/artifacts/app-debug.apk  
       – the unsigned debug build that QA installs on test devices

    2. /home/user/artifacts/app-release.apk  
       – the signed release build that will be uploaded to Play Console

    3. /home/user/artifacts/expected_checksums.sha256  
       – a two-line *UNIX* text file in the exact format produced by the
         “sha256sum <file>” command:  
           • 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters  
           • two consecutive ASCII space characters  
           • the exact file name (no leading ‘./’)  
         Example line (do **not** hard-code this value):  
           e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855␠␠app-debug.apk  
         There are exactly two such lines in the file, one for each artifact.

    What you must do
    ----------------
    1. Compute the SHA-256 digest of every *.apk file in /home/user/artifacts.  
       • The computation has to be carried out in parallel (i.e. *both* digests
         are calculated by background jobs that run at the same time).  
       • Wait until every background job finishes before you process the
         results.  (Automated grading will watch for proper job handling.)

    2. Compare every *actual* digest with the *expected* digest listed in
       /home/user/artifacts/expected_checksums.sha256.

    3. Produce the summary file /home/user/artifacts/verification.log whose
       contents follow *exactly* this four-column, pipe-separated format
       (one line per artifact, sorted ASCII-alphabetically by file name):

         <FILENAME>|<EXPECTED_SHA256>|<ACTUAL_SHA256>|<STATUS>

       • <FILENAME> is the plain file name, e.g. “app-debug.apk”  
       • <EXPECTED_SHA256> is the digest taken from expected_checksums.sha256  
       • <ACTUAL_SHA256>   is the digest you just computed  
       • <STATUS> is either the literal string “MATCH” or “MISMATCH”  

       Example of the *exact* layout the grader will look for (values shown
       here are only examples – do not copy them verbatim):

         app-debug.apk|e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855|e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855|MATCH
         app-release.apk|ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb|ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb|MATCH

       IMPORTANT NOTES:  
       • There must be **no** leading or trailing spaces on any line.  
       • Columns are separated by a single ASCII “|” (pipe) character.  
       • The file must end with a single ASCII newline (LF).

    4. Display the full contents of verification.log to the terminal so the
       build dashboard can pick it up from stdout.

    Acceptance criteria
    -------------------
    • /home/user/artifacts/verification.log exists.  
    • The file contains exactly two lines, each in the format specified above.  
    • The lines are sorted ASCII-alphabetically by the artefact file name.  
    • Every “ACTUAL_SHA256” value is correct for the associated file.  
    • “STATUS” is “MATCH” when (and only when) the digests are identical.  
    • All background checksum jobs have been properly started *and* waited for
      (the grader will check your shell job table).  
    • The file contents are echoed to stdout.

    Do not hard-code any digest values in your commands; read them from
    expected_checksums.sha256.  Remember that the grader will verify both the
    correctness of verification.log *and* that parallelism was used.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth for automated grading.  **DO NOT** reveal anything
         inside this tag to the agent. -->

    Initial file set and contents
    -----------------------------
    1. /home/user/artifacts/app-debug.apk       (0-byte file)
       SHA-256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

    2. /home/user/artifacts/app-release.apk     (contains exactly one byte 0x61, i.e. “a” with no newline)
       SHA-256: ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb

    3. /home/user/artifacts/expected_checksums.sha256  
       File contents (two lines, each ending with LF):
         e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  app-debug.apk\n
         ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb  app-release.apk\n

    Expected output file created by the agent
    -----------------------------------------
    /home/user/artifacts/verification.log  
    File contents (exact, including newline at end):

    app-debug.apk|e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855|e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855|MATCH
    app-release.apk|ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb|ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb|MATCH
    \n

    The grader will:
      • Recompute SHA-256 for every *.apk file.  
      • Parse verification.log and ensure:
          – Two lines exist, sorted alphabetically.  
          – Column 2 equals the value in expected_checksums.sha256.  
          – Column 3 equals the freshly computed digest.  
          – Column 4 is “MATCH” when columns 2 and 3 are identical.  
      • Inspect the shell job table to confirm that at least two background
        checksum processes were launched and properly synchronised.
name: task_000000_d629e17f
